State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5862          322-5860          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


August 22, 1980


Assemblyman Don Rogers
State Capitol, RM. 4160
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Assemblyman Rogers:

This is in response to a request from your office that
we confirm in writing the advice which we provided over the
telephone shortly after receiving your letter to Tom Houston
dated March 27, 1980.

In that letter you asked whether you incurred any
reporting obligation by virtue of accepting air transpor-
tation from the Salyer Land Company to a testimonial dinner
for Assemblywoman Carol Hallett. Salyer reported the cost
of flying you and several other legislators to the dinner
as an in-kind contribution to the Committee to Re-Elect
Assemblywoman Hallett, and the Committee reported the re-
ceipt of an in-kind contribution in that amount on their
campaign report.

Because the event which you attended was a political
one, and because the Salyer Land Company did not include a
vacation or otherwise provide you with any benefits which
were not incidental to your attendance at the political
event, the payment by Salyer was correctly characterized as
a contribution, reportable pursuant to Chapter 4 of the
Political Reform Act, rather than a gift which would have
been reportable on your Statement of Economic Interests
pursuant to Chapter 7 of the Act.  See Government Code
Section 82028(b)(4) and the FPPC Opinion, 1 FPPC Ops. 137
(No. 75-094-C, 1975).

Furthermore, we agree that the contribution was made
to the Committee to Re-Elect Assemblywoman Hallett rather
than to the individual legislators who were flown to the

Assemblyman Don Rogers
August 22, 1980
Page Two


dinner. To the extent you received any payment by accepting
something of value, i.e., the transportation, you provided
adequate consideration by your attendance at the event,
Cory, supra, at 139. You therefore incurred no reporting
obligation under Chapter 4 either.

     If you have any further questions concerning this matter,
please do not hesitate to call me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Sarah Cameron
                                        Deputy Chief for
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Legal Division

JOINT RULES COMMITTEE
COMMISSION ON GENETIC DISEASES
ASSEMBLY SELECT COMMITTEE

LAMENTO ADDRESS: 1160
STE CAPITOL, ROOM [illegible]
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
PHONE (916) 445-[illegible]

DISTRICT OFFICE
2. 14TH STREET
BAKERSFIELD, CA 93301
TELEPHONE (805) 323-4017

Assembly
California Legislature

DON ROGERS
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-THIRD DISTRICT

March 27, 1980

Mr. Thomas Houston
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Houston:

The enclosed copy of a letter I received from Assemblywoman
Carol Hallett which came to my attention after I had filed my
Statement of Economic Interest report raises a question.

As the letter from Mrs. Hallett states, several people including
myself flew in a Salyer Land Company plane from Sacramento to
Santa Maria to attend a testimonial dinner February 14, 1979.

The Salyer people reported the use of their company plane as
a $700 in-kind contribution to the Committee to Reelect Assemblywoman
Hallett. No reference was made to any other legislator in the Salyer
Statement of Economic Interest report.

My question to you is: Should I also show this contribution
on my report?

Please let me know at your earliest convenience so that I will
be able to act one way or the other and settle the matter.

Yours truly,

DON ROGERS

DR:BC

ECL.

Assembly
California Legislature

DON ROGERS
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-THIRD DISTRICT

March 27, 1980

Mr. Thomas Houston
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Houston:

The enclosed copy of a letter I received from Assemblywoman
Carol Hallett which came to my attention after I had filed my
Statement of Economic Interest report raises a question.

As the letter from Mrs. Hallett states, several people including
myself flew in a Salyer Land Company plane round trip Sacramento to
Santa Maria to attend a testimonial dinner February 14, 1979.

The Salyer people reported the use of their company plane as
a $700 in-kind contribution to the Committee to Re-elect Assemblywoman
Hallett. No reference was made to any other legislator in the Salyer
Statement of Economic Interest report.

My question to you is: Should I also show this contribution
on my report?

Please let me know at your earliest convenience so that I will
be able to act one way or the other and settle the matter.

Yours truly,

DON ROGERS

DR:BC

ECL.

In that letter you asked whether you incurred any reporting obligation by virtue of accepting air transportation from the Salyer Land Company to a testimonial dinner for Assemblywoman Carol Hallett. Salyer reported the cost of flying you and several other legislators to the dinner as an in-kind contribution to the Committee to Re-Elect Assemblywoman Hallett, and the Committee reported the receipt of an in-kind contribution in that amount on their campaign report. Because the event which you attended was a political one, and because the Salyer Land Company did not include a vacation or otherwise provide you with any benefits which were not incidental to your attendance at the political event, the payment by Salyer was correctly characterized as a contribution, reportable pursuant to Chapter 4 of the Political Reform Act, rather than a gift which would have been reportable on your Statement of Economic Interests pursuant to Chapter 7 of the Act. See Government Code Section 82028(b)(4) and the Cory Opinion, 1 FFPC Ops. 137 (No. 75-094-C, 1975). Furthermore, we agree that the contribution was made to the Committee to Re-Elect Assemblywoman Hallett rather than to the individual legislators who were flown to the Assemblyman Don Rogers August 22, 1980 Page Two dinner. To the extent you received any payment by accepting something of value, i.e., the transportation, you provided adequate consideration by your attendance at the event. Cory, supra, at 139. You therefore incurred no reporting obligation under Chapter 4 either. If you have any further questions concerning this matter, please do not hesitate to call me.
